summary: Tested two v6 features end-to-end: (A) Strict moderation on POST /api/posts and CreatePostModal inline warning. (B) Persistent music playback via global MiniPlayer across SPA navigation. All 37 backend pytests pass (26 regression + 11 new moderation cases). Frontend flows for unsafe-warning gating, safe post submission, Spotify/YouTube playback, MiniPlayer persistence across routes, toggle collapse/expand, and close all behave per spec. Music share modal and explicit badges on confession posts also verified visually.
action_items: OPTIONAL/CODE-REVIEW: MiniPlayer.jsx renders two SEPARATE <iframe> elements depending on `expanded` state (lines 76-96 when expanded, 100-121 when collapsed). Toggling between collapsed/expanded therefore unmounts the visible iframe and mounts a hidden one (or vice versa), which restarts audio playback. The spec explicitly says 'collapse the embed iframe (without unmounting it — audio keeps playing)'. Consider always rendering a single iframe and only toggling its visibility/size with CSS (display:none + width/height 0, or absolute positioning off-screen) so audio truly continues. Navigation-level persistence is fine because the MiniPlayer component itself stays mounted in App.js — that test passed.
critical_code_review_comments: MiniPlayer.jsx: collapse/expand uses two different iframe elements -> audio restarts on toggle (spec violation, see action_items).

Spotify embed throws client-side `RangeError: Incorrect locale information provided` from embed-cdn.spotifycdn.com — third-party issue, not blocking, but worth tracking if it affects users in some locales.
test_moderation_v6.py — request body uses `device_id` field (not `X-Device-ID` header). Aligned with existing test_pluto_api.py pattern; documented for future agents.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create transient TEST_ posts via /api/posts using fresh device_ids; auto-vanish in 24h.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend has 37 pytests across 3 files in /app/backend/tests/. Always pass REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env or sourcing /app/frontend/.env. Moderation tests live in test_moderation_v6.py — parametrized over UNSAFE and SAFE phrase lists. POST /api/posts requires body field `device_id` (not header). The MiniPlayer single-iframe refactor (if main agent applies the action_item) should be re-tested by toggling mini-player-toggle and confirming the iframe element identity (e.g. via persistence marker) survives.
rca of the issue: No critical issues found. The single code-review note about MiniPlayer collapse iframe identity is a robustness improvement, not a user-visible failure during the tested navigation regression (navigation between /, /music, /topics keeps the iframe alive because expanded state defaults to true and never changes during navigation).
